meta.description: High-signal items only: contracts, earnings, funding, IPO signals, defense awards, acquisitions. urgency: immediate=act now, same_day=review today, watch=monitor

immediate.title: SpaceX wins $4 billion Space Force contract for satellites that target 'airborne threats' anywhere on Earth
immediate.summary: The U.S Space Force has awarded SpaceX $4.16 billion to help the military track airborne targets using satellites.

immediate.title: GE, Rolls-Royce Get Contracts to Advance Autonomous Drone Engine Designs
immediate.summary: The Air Force has awarded contracts to General Electric Aerospace and Rolls-Royce to further hone their designs for engines that would power medium-thrust autonomous drones. The post GE, Rolls-Royce Get Contracts to Advance Autonomous Drone Engine Designs appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

The first pathfinder mission is planned for delivery in 2028. immediate.title: KC-46’s New Remote Vision System Completes Initial ‘Non-Contact’ Testing
immediate.summary: Boeing has completed initial flight testing on its redesigned Remote Vision System for the KC-46 tanker, the defense giant announced June 4. The new system, dubbed RVS 2.0, is meant to replace the original camera and video system boom operators use to refuel other aircraft, which has faced issues for years now. The post KC-46’s New Remote Vision System Completes Initial ‘Non-Contact’ Testing appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

immediate.title: US Reduces Forces Committed to NATO, Tells Allies to Step Up Air and Naval Power
immediate.summary: The U.S. is reducing the number of forces it is prepared to commit to NATO in a crisis, U.S. European Command said in a June 3 announcement. The changes, which were previewed to alliance members last month by a senior Pentagon official, appear to include a decrease in the number of combat aircraft, air-to-air refueling planes, and Navy vessels. The post US Reduces Forces Committed to NATO, Tells Allies to Step Up Air and Naval Power appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

immediate.title: New Push for Separate ‘Cyber Force’ Builds, but Questions Remain
immediate.summary: The idea to establish a new military service dedicated to cyber warfare reared its head again this week thanks to a proposal in Congress and a think tank report. But essential questions about how a so-called “Cyber Force” would be manned and work with the other services remain. The post New Push for Separate Cyber Force Builds, but Questions Remain appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

The spacecraft was heard last on Dec. 6, when it experienced an unexpected loss of signal after []

immediate.title: Blue Origin Rocket Explosion the Largest Ever at Cape Canaveral: Commander
immediate.summary: The May 28 explosion of a Blue Origin rocket at Cape Canaveral Space Force Station, Fla., is the largest the installation has seen in its history, according to the commander of the range. Despite the unprecedented nature of the anomaly, the base’s safety precautions before and after the incident helped contain its impact on personnel and on the broader installation. The post Blue Origin Rocket Explosion the Largest Ever at Cape Canaveral: Commander appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

immediate.title: As Fighter Fleet Shrinks Below Legal Minimum, Pfluger Sounds Alarm
immediate.summary: The Air Force’s primary fighter fleet dipped below the minimum allowable size under law earlier this year—and that fact should rally a “call to action” for the nation to reinvest in its airpower, a key lawmaker said June 2. The post As Fighter Fleet Shrinks Below Legal Minimum, Pfluger Sounds Alarm appeared first on Air & Space Forces Magazine .

same_day.title: Registration open for Aerospace Test & Development Show 2026
same_day.summary: Registration for the Aerospace Test & Development Show 2026 at Hall 7, MEETT, Toulouse, is now open. The show takes place on 29–30 September 2026. The Aerospace Test & Development Show is the worlds leading and largest event 100% dedicated to aircraft, drone, helicopter, space and defence testing and development, and is organised by the publishers of Aerospace Testing International. This years edition will be the most comprehensive to date. With more than 150 exhibitors expected and over 70 speakers confirmed, the 2026 show will feature three free-to-attend forum stages: the Testing Forum, the Defence Testing Forum, and the Future Propulsion Forum — giving attendees more opportunities than e

same_day.title: How new FAA rules are opening the skies to personal eVTOLs
same_day.summary: Imagine operating a personal VTOL from your driveway and transitioning seamlessly into open skies. With the FAA’s Modernization of Special Airworthiness Certification ruling, the dream of taking off from your home, avoiding traffic and flying directly to your destination is a step closer to reality. The MOSAIC rule changes how VTOLs can be certified for personal use. The ruling, finalized in July last year, removes older maximum weight limits for Light Sport Aircraft (LSA) in favor of performance-based metrics, paving the way for owner-operated VTOLs. Continue reading How new FAA rules are opening the skies to personal eVTOLs at Aerospace Testing International.
